title: Neottia fugongensis

Within the plant kingdom, its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales. intro: Neottia fugongensis, also known as Fugong's Tongue Orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Yunnan Province in China. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Neottia fugongensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neottia fugongensis is often prepared as a dried powder or decoction, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues.
